pi_items.source: Task description section 4: 'Save the project as /home/ga/Documents/projects/structural_design.wpa'
Task description section 4: 'Create a text file at `/home/ga/Documents/blade_mass.txt`'
Task description section 2: 'Create a new blade named StructBlade10'
Task description section 3: Mass values at 0m=40 kg/m, 5m=20 kg/m, 10m=5 kg/m
Task description section 3: Stiffness at 0m=1.0e7, 5m=0.5e7, 10m=0.1e7 (equivalent to 10000000, 5000000, 1000000)
Calculated from structural values using trapezoidal rule: section 0-5m = (40+20)/2 * 5 = 150 kg; section 5-10m = (20+5)/2 * 5 = 62.5 kg; total = 212.5 kg. Verified mathematically.
Tolerance value set by task creator for acceptable mass variation (±2.5 kg from target of 212.5 kg). Reasonable value for floating-point calculations and interpolation methods.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic wind turbine blade design task requiring interaction with QBlade software. The task specifies creation of a blade named 'StructBlade10' with three sections at 0m, 5m, and 10m positions. Structural properties are: mass per unit length of [40, 20, 5] kg/m and stiffness (EI) of [10000000, 5000000, 1000000] (representing [1.0e7, 0.5e7, 0.1e7]). The calculated total blade mass using trapezoidal integration is 212.5 kg. The blade uses the NACA 0012 airfoil (a well-known symmetrical airfoil with 12% thickness-to-chord ratio used extensively in rotor blade applications). The agent must interface with QBlade's Blade Design module to define both aerodynamic (airfoil) and structural (mass and stiffness distribution) properties, then extract the computed total blade mass and save the project file.
